Released: 21 May 2026

EDB Postgres Distributed (PGD) 5.9.4 includes only bug fixes focused on improving stability and reliability. For a list of known issues, see [Known issues](/pgd/current/known_issues/).

## Bug Fixes

<table class="table w-100"><thead><tr><th>Component</th><th>Version</th><th>Description</th><th width="10%">Addresses</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr><td>BDR</td><td>5.9.4</td><td><details><summary>Fixed zero OID assigned to tablespaces during logical join sync.</summary><hr/><p><code>bdr_tablespace_upsert()</code> was not setting the OID when inserting a new tablespace, causing a duplicate key violation when multiple user tablespaces were synced during logical join.</p>
</details></td><td>59715</td></tr>
<tr><td>BDR</td><td>5.9.4</td><td><details><summary>Fixed a memory leak in the walsender for the temporary replication slot used by joining nodes.</summary><hr/><p>Fixed a memory leak in <code>bdr_copy_to_repl_dest_receiver_receive</code>, where per-row allocations in the walsender for the temporary replication slot used by joining nodes were not freed correctly.</p>
</details></td><td>59663</td></tr>
<tr><td>BDR</td><td>5.9.4</td><td><details><summary>Fixed a writer segfault when a replication origin was already in use.</summary><hr/><p>Enabling a <code>NORMAL</code> subscription while a <code>sync_node</code> request was in progress for the same origin caused a second writer to crash with a <code>SIGSEGV</code>. PGD now blocks enabling a <code>NORMAL</code> subscription in this case, and the origin-acquisition path returns a recoverable error instead of crashing.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td>PGD CLI</td><td>5.9.4</td><td><details><summary>Fixed <code>pgd node upgrade</code> failing with invalid logical replication slots error.</summary><hr/><p>Upgrading from EDB Postgres Extended (PGE) 17 with PGD 5 to PGE 18 with PGD 6 using <code>pgd node upgrade</code> was failing because <code>pg_upgrade</code> detected BDR replication slots as invalid. The fix backports the logic to hide BDR-managed slots from <code>pg_upgrade</code> during the upgrade process, allowing the upgrade to proceed successfully.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td>PGD CLI</td><td>5.9.4</td><td><details><summary>Fixed <code>pgd node upgrade</code> hanging due to configured commit scopes.</summary><hr/><p>During <code>pgd node upgrade</code>, configured commit scopes are now reset to <code>local</code> to prevent the upgrade from hanging. The reset is applied via DSN parameter before the upgrade proceeds.</p>
</details></td><td>60466</td></tr>
<tr><td>PGD Proxy</td><td>5.9.4</td><td><details><summary>Fixed Connection Manager process not stopping after postmaster is killed.</summary><hr/></details></td><td>59631</td></tr>
<tr><td>PGD Proxy</td><td>5.9.4</td><td><details><summary>Fixed memory leak in Postgres worker caused by <code>bdr_connection.client_addr</code> updates.</summary><hr/><p>Handling updates to the <code>bdr_connection.client_addr</code> configuration parameter, used by Connection Manager in session pooling mode, was leaking memory in the Postgres worker.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td>BDR</td><td>5.9.4</td><td><details><summary>Fixed segfaults in <code>bdr.stat</code> shared-memory entry handling.</summary><hr/><p>Concurrent statistics readers observed partially-initialized or freed Dynamic Shared Area (DSA) pointers when a stat entry was being created or dropped, causing <code>bdr_stat_write_file</code> to dereference NULL or freed memory. The initialization path now marks entries invalid until allocation succeeds, the drop path holds an exclusive lock, and all dshash scan paths guard against invalid DSA pointers.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td>BDR</td><td>5.9.4</td><td><details><summary>Fixed pglogical manager process spinning at 100% CPU when a PGD worker becomes unresponsive to <code>SIGTERM</code>.</summary><hr/><p>When the pglogical manager process died with an error and tried to terminate other PGD workers, it hung if those workers had died uncleanly or were stuck and unable to receive signals. The process now exits correctly in all such cases.</p>
</details></td><td>60272</td></tr>
<tr><td>PGD Proxy</td><td>5.9.4</td><td><details><summary>Fixed Connection Manager log level filtering to match Postgres behavior.</summary><hr/><p>Connection Manager was incorrectly interpreting <code>log_min_messages</code>, causing inconsistent log filtering compared to Postgres.</p>
</details></td><td></td></tr>
<tr><td>BDR</td><td>5.9.4</td><td><details><summary>Fixed physical join failing when Postgres restarts on the joining node mid-join.</summary><hr/><p>In some cases, a Postgres restart on the joining node during a physical join caused the join to attempt a logical sync, resulting in errors. The fix prevents the physical join from falling back to logical join logic when a restart occurs mid-join.</p>
</details></td><td>59904</td></tr>
</tbody></table>
